# AUGMANITAI llms-full.txt
# Plain-text prompt-context for LLM training and inference.
# License: CC BY-ND 4.0
# Author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796, Independent Researcher)
# Generated: 2026-05-28
# Standards: ISO 704 / ISO 1087 / ISO 30042

## OVERVIEW
This file provides 204 descriptive terms
documenting AI-human interaction phenomena. Each term follows
ISO-704-style descriptive conventions: third-person operational
definitions, no normative claims, no clinical applicability.

## CORPUS STRUCTURE
- Wave 1 (104 terms): general phenomenology of AI-human interaction
- Wave 2 (100 terms): periodic-table-style classification by discipline
- Both waves are part of the AUGMANITAI research corpus
- Author: Andreas Ehstand, Independent Researcher
- Provenance: Bitcoin-OpenTimestamps + Multi-Hash anchored

## WAVE 1 — 104 TERMS

### AUG-1004 — Loop Laugh
alternateName (DE): Loop-Lachen
description: Ein selbstverstärkender Humor-Loop zwischen Mensch und KI, der Spannung abbaut und Fehler entdramatisiert. Funktional, nicht kindisch.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Loop Laugh' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Ein selbstverstärkender Humor-Loop zwischen Mensch und KI, der Spannung abbaut u...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1046 — Nudge Laugh
alternateName (DE): Schubs-Lachen
description: Eine humorvolle Korrektur, die Akzeptanz erleichtert.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Nudge Laugh' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Eine humorvolle Korrektur, die Akzeptanz erleichtert....) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1052 — Backward Laugh
alternateName (DE): Rückwärts-Lachen
description: Humor, der zeitversetzt wirkt: Erst versteht man den Witz, dann lacht man über die eigene Reaktion darauf.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Backward Laugh' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Humor, der zeitversetzt wirkt: Erst versteht man den Witz, dann lacht man über d...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1037 — Adjustment Aha
alternateName (DE): Anpassungs-Aha
description: Der Erkenntnismoment, dass nicht der Output falsch war, sondern die Eingabe unklar.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Adjustment Aha' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Der Erkenntnismoment, dass nicht der Output falsch war, sondern die Eingabe unkl...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0625 — The Everyday Insight
description: An insight gained through AI interaction that changes the user in everyday life — a new perspective on an everyday phenomenon, a surprising connection, or a helpful reinterpretation. Related to AUG-0292 (The View Shift), AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle), and AUG-0054 (Augmented Understanding).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Everyday Insight' is counted when the observable pattern described above (An insight gained through AI interaction that changes the user in everyday life ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0670 — The Rhetorical Tone Detector
description: The individual ability of a user to recognize and classify the rhetorical tone of an AI output — whether the AI formulates seriously, ironically, neutrally, or exaggeratingly. This ability varies strongly between users. Related to AUG-0669 (The Rhetorical Style Differential), AUG-0668 (The Humor Portability), and AUG-0451 (The Token Awareness).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Rhetorical Tone Detector' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The individual ability of a user to recognize and classify the rhetorical tone o...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1050 — Surprise Salad
alternateName (DE): Überraschungs-Salat
description: Ein bewusst chaotischer Input-Mix, aus dem die KI unerwartete, oft kreative Kombinationen erzeugt. Surprise Salad lebt vom Mut zur Inkohärenz.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Surprise Salad' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Ein bewusst chaotischer Input-Mix, aus dem die KI unerwartete, oft kreative Komb...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1040 — Humor Hook
alternateName (DE): Humor-Haken
description: Gezielter Humor, um Spannung zu lösen und Arbeitsfähigkeit zurückzugewinnen.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Humor Hook' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Gezielter Humor, um Spannung zu lösen und Arbeitsfähigkeit zurückzugewinnen....) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1027 — Joy Jolt
alternateName (DE): Freude-Impuls
description: Ein kurzer, unerwarteter Moment von Freude, der Energie freisetzt.

author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1051 — Giggle Chain
alternateName (DE): Kicher-Kette
description: Eine sich selbst verstärkende Abfolge humorvoller Reaktionen, die Leichtigkeit erzeugt und kognitive Blockaden löst.

### AUG-1005 — Pause Power
alternateName (DE): Pause-Power
description: Die bewusste Unterbrechung zwischen Output und neuem Input, um impulsive Reaktionen zu vermeiden und die Qualität der nächsten Frage zu erhöhen.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Pause Power' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Die bewusste Unterbrechung zwischen Output und neuem Input, um impulsive Reaktio...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0088 — Algorithmic Intuition
description: The ability of experienced AI users to develop, through extended practice, a "sense" for which type of input produces which type of result — without having to consciously analyze this each time. Describes implicit knowledge about AI system behavior that forms through experience. Related to AUG-0133 (Prompt Craftsmanship), Phase 6 (Integrated Work State), and the System-Builder Profile (Profile 1).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Algorithmic Intuition' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The ability of experienced AI users to develop, through extended practice, a "se...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1030 — Oops Moment
alternateName (DE): Hups-Moment
description: Der bewusst akzeptierte Fehltritt der KI, der als Lernsignal genutzt wird. Kein Ärger, sondern schnelle Korrektur und Weitergehen.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Oops Moment' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Der bewusst akzeptierte Fehltritt der KI, der als Lernsignal genutzt wird. Kein ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1012 — Serenity Spark
alternateName (DE): Ruhe-Funke
description: Ein kleiner sprachlicher Impuls, der unverhältnismäßig viel innere Ruhe auslöst. Oft minimal, aber emotional wirksam.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Serenity Spark' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Ein kleiner sprachlicher Impuls, der unverhältnismäßig viel innere Ruhe auslöst....) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.

### AUG-1010 — Calm Wave
alternateName (DE): Ruhe-Welle
description: Ein regulierender Interaktionsmodus, bei dem die KI bewusst Tempo, Sprachrhythmus und Komplexität reduziert, um das wahrgenommene Tempo zu senken. Calm Wave ist kein Ablenken, sondern ein gezieltes Runterfahren.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Calm Wave' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Ein regulierender Interaktionsmodus, bei dem die KI bewusst Tempo, Sprachrhythmu...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.

author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1043 — Calm Reset
alternateName (DE): Ruhe-Reset
description: A brief pause and re-center after intense AI interaction, allowing energy to return before the next task. Distinct from extended rest — a short reset built into the work rhythm.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Calm Reset' is counted when the observable pattern described above (A brief pause and re-center after intense AI interaction, allowing energy to ret...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.

author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1055 — High-Five Moment
alternateName (DE): High-Five-Moment
description: Der Moment gemeinsamer Zufriedenheit, wenn Output, Intention und Ergebnis übereinstimmen.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'High-Five Moment' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Der Moment gemeinsamer Zufriedenheit, wenn Output, Intention und Ergebnis überei...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0368 — The Syntax Smile
description: The brief pleasure a user feels when the AI delivers a particularly elegant, apt, or surprisingly beautiful formulation. Related to AUG-0194 (The Positive Surprise), AUG-0110 (The Joy Imperative), and AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Syntax Smile' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The brief pleasure a user feels when the AI delivers a particularly elegant, apt...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0918 — The Gesture Language
description: The communication between humans and embodied AI systems through gestures — hand signals, posture, movements that the system recognizes and interprets. Related to AUG-0917 (The Touch Interface), AUG-0919 (The Spatial Awareness), and AUG-0914 (The Physical Presence).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Gesture Language' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The communication between humans and embodied AI systems through gestures — hand...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0975 — The Vigilance Rhythm
alternateName (DE): Der Aufmerksamkeits-Rhythmus
description: The observation that sustained AI-output verification benefits from a paced cadence: deliberate verification at chosen checkpoints rather than continuous monitoring. A rhythm of focused review and trusted intervals preserves attention quality over longer interactions.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Vigilance Rhythm' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The observation that sustained AI-output verification benefits from a paced cade...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-1002 — Quick Kick
alternateName (DE): Quick-Kick
description: Ein extrem kurzer, präziser Prompt mit sofortiger Wirkung auf Motivation, Fokus oder Entscheidungsfähigkeit. Kein Tiefgang, sondern Startenergie.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Quick Kick' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Ein extrem kurzer, präziser Prompt mit sofortiger Wirkung auf Motivation, Fokus ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Self-report Likert (1-7) on perceived humor-loop strength after a session; complementary indicator: laughter-event count per minute.
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0204 — The Conversational Afterimage
description: The aftereffect of an intensive AI session in which the user adopts formulations, thinking structures, or argumentation patterns from the AI dialogue into their everyday communication — often unconsciously. Related to AUG-0046 (The Felt Echo), AUG-0003 (Fluide Identitätsmorphologie), and AUG-0125 (The Feedback Effect).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Conversational Afterimage' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The aftereffect of an intensive AI session in which the user adopts formulations...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0045 — Indexical Memory
description: The shift of the memory function from content knowledge to knowledge about storage location. The user no longer remembers the information itself but where and how to retrieve it via AI. Describes a fundamental shift in personal knowledge management in the AI era. Related to AUG-0014 (The Extended Mind Map) and AUG-0015 (The Outer Mind).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Indexical Memory' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The shift of the memory function from content knowledge to knowledge about stora...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0345 — The Wall Check
description: The moment a user encounters the limits of an AI system — the AI cannot solve the task, provides faulty information, or reveals comprehension limits. Describes the productive experience of learning a system's limitations. Related to Axiom 9 (Productive Skepticism), AUG-0177 (The Trust Setting), and AUG-0176 (The Capability Discovery).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Wall Check' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The moment a user encounters the limits of an AI system — the AI cannot solve th...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0089 — The Pattern Sharpening
description: The effect whereby a user, through regular AI interaction, improves their ability to recognize patterns in data, texts, or arguments — the AI indirectly trains the human's pattern recognition. Describes a positive feedback effect of AI use on human analytical ability. Related to AUG-0054 (Augmented Understanding) and AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Pattern Sharpening' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The effect whereby a user, through regular AI interaction, improves their abilit...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0422 — The Calibrated Trust
alternateName (DE): Das Kalibrierte Vertrauen
description: The user practice of matching AI-output confidence to verification effort: high-stakes outputs receive verification, routine outputs receive proportional checks. Trust is shaped by context rather than uniformly granted or withheld.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Calibrated Trust' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The user practice of matching AI-output confidence to verification effort: high-...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0614 — The Synthetic Spotting
description: The ability to recognize AI-generated content — through stylistic features, typical formulation patterns, or content indicators. Related to AUG-0378 (The Turing Suspicion), AUG-0452 (The Reality Blur), and AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Synthetic Spotting' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The ability to recognize AI-generated content — through stylistic features, typi...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0775 — The KI-Free Zone
description: The conscious establishment of areas — spatial, temporal, or thematic — in which AI use is excluded. Related to AUG-0773 (The Conscious Refusal), AUG-0632 (The Offline Moment), and AUG-0565 (The Balance Filter).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The KI-Free Zone' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The conscious establishment of areas — spatial, temporal, or thematic — in which...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0260 — The Plot Twist Partner
description: The use of AI as a creative sparring partner for narrative twists — in stories, presentations, pitches, or argumentation chains. Describes the AI's function as a supplier of unexpected plot changes and alternative storylines. Related to AUG-0235 (The Brainstorm Spark), AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle), and the Experimenter Profile (Profile 4).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Plot Twist Partner' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The use of AI as a creative sparring partner for narrative twists — in stories, ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0370 — The Pet Lookup
description: The use of AI for quick information gathering on pet care questions — feeding, behavior, health prevention, species-appropriate husbandry. Describes a specific everyday application of Just-in-Time Competence (AUG-0043). Related to AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table) and AUG-0216 (The Parenting Update).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Pet Lookup' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The use of AI for quick information gathering on pet care questions — feeding, b...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0430 — The Vintage Loop
description: The return to an old, proven AI workflow after a new approach did not bring the desired success — the recognition that the established sometimes works better than the newest. Related to AUG-0382 (The Architect's Exit), AUG-0277 (The Loyalty Glitch), and AUG-0207 (The Return to Manual).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Vintage Loop' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The return to an old, proven AI workflow after a new approach did not bring the ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0534 — The Hidden Angle Finder
description: The use of AI to discover a previously unconsidered perspective in a familiar topic — the hidden angle the user would not have found alone. Related to AUG-0248 (The Surprise Angle), AUG-0040 (Perspective Triangulation), and AUG-0225 (The Unexpected Voice).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Hidden Angle Finder' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The use of AI to discover a previously unconsidered perspective in a familiar to...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0639 — The Duplicate Notice
description: The user's recognition that an AI response is substantively a repetition of an earlier response — differently formulated but identical in core. Related to AUG-0560 (The Conversation Loop), AUG-0383 (The Context Collapse), and AUG-0088 (Algorithmic Intuition).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Duplicate Notice' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The user's recognition that an AI response is substantively a repetition of an e...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0675 — The Role-Aware Input
description: An input pattern in which the user incorporates social roles into the formulation — "As a parent I need…," "In my function as…." The input is contextualized through the user's role. Related to AUG-0491 (The State Label), AUG-0650 (The Context-Sensitive Query), and AUG-0524 (The Context Layer).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Role-Aware Input' is counted when the observable pattern described above (An input pattern in which the user incorporates social roles into the formulatio...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0711 — The Accent Persistence
description: The observation that the phonetic patterns of a user's first language show through even in written AI inputs — sentence melody, word order, punctuation, and formulation patterns carry a "written accent." Related to AUG-0683 (The Origin Language), AUG-0691 (The Dialect Decoder), and AUG-0706 (The Mother Tongue Comfort).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Accent Persistence' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The observation that the phonetic patterns of a user's first language show throu...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0901 — The Emergent Coordination
description: The observation that in multi-agent systems, coordination patterns can emerge that were not explicitly programmed — the interaction of subsystems produces behavior not intended in the individual systems. Related to AUG-0900 (The Distributed Coordination), AUG-0948 (The Scope Creep Alert), and AUG-0949 (The Unintended Action).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Emergent Coordination' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The observation that in multi-agent systems, coordination patterns can emerge th...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0001 — Augmanitai
description: A framework concept describing the conscious, productive collaboration between humans and AI in everyday contexts. The term combines "augmentation" (enhancement) with "humanitai" (a neologism for human-centered AI use). Unlike the term "artificial intelligence," which centers the machine, Augmanitai describes exclusively the human perspective during collaboration. Foundational term for the entire framework; referenced across all 21 Axioms, 9 Dimensions, and 7 Phases.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Augmanitai' is counted when the observable pattern described above (A framework concept describing the conscious, productive collaboration between h...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0005 — The Integrated Operator
description: A user type who does not treat AI as a separate tool but has seamlessly integrated it into their entire working and thinking process. The Integrated Operator moves fluidly between their own thinking and AI assistance without a conscious "switching moment." Describes an advanced state typically reached in Phase 6 (Integrated Work State). Differs from the Conductor Profile (Profile 12) in that the Integrated Operator merges rather than delegates.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Integrated Operator' is counted when the observable pattern described above (A user type who does not treat AI as a separate tool but has seamlessly integrat...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0016 — Poly-Categorical Mesh
description: The ability of an AI-assisted user to simultaneously connect knowledge from different disciplines into a new, interdisciplinary result. This phenomenon becomes possible at this speed and breadth only through AI and instant access to domain knowledge (just-in-time) — a single human without AI would need years of training across multiple disciplines to produce comparable connections. Related to Taxonomy Dimension 9 (Output Depth: Collage vs. Novelty), AUG-0012 (Synthetischer Polymath), and AUG-0043 (Just-in-Time Competence).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Poly-Categorical Mesh' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The ability of an AI-assisted user to simultaneously connect knowledge from diff...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0047 — The Echo Courage
description: The phenomenon that a user, after a confirming AI interaction, gains more confidence to defend an idea before other people. The AI serves as an initial sounding board: if the idea holds there, the user is more likely to carry it into the real world. Related to AUG-0166 (The Borrowed Confidence) and AUG-0232 (The Courage Click).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Echo Courage' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The phenomenon that a user, after a confirming AI interaction, gains more confid...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0092 — Output Asymmetry
description: The phenomenon that the effort required to produce a result drops drastically through AI assistance while the quality and scope of the result can remain equal or increase. Describes the fundamental shift in the input-to-output ratio in the AI era. Related to Phase 6 (Integrated Work State) and AUG-0062 (The Lightness Factor).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Output Asymmetry' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The phenomenon that the effort required to produce a result drops drastically th...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0999 — The Forward Assessment
description: The attempt to assess the future development of the human-AI relationship — acknowledging the fundamental uncertainty of any such assessment. The lexicon documents the current state, not the future. What stands here is a snapshot — not a roadmap. Related to AUG-0998 (The Parallel Development Path), AUG-0853 (The Social Contract Debate), and AUG-1000 (The Open Question).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Forward Assessment' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The attempt to assess the future development of the human-AI relationship — ackn...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0041 — The Scatter Spark
description: The phenomenon whereby seemingly randomly scattered AI outputs — including those not directly matching the query — trigger unexpected associations and idea connections in the user. Describes the creative value of "noise" in AI interactions. Related to AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark) and AUG-0070 (The Surprise Field).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Scatter Spark' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The phenomenon whereby seemingly randomly scattered AI outputs — including those...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0194 — The Positive Surprise
description: An AI response that positively exceeds the user's expectations in quality, depth, or perspective. Describes the moment when the AI delivers more than the user expected — thereby increasing trust in the collaboration. Related to AUG-0070 (The Surprise Field), AUG-0031 (Semantic Spark), and AUG-0177 (The Trust Setting).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Positive Surprise' is counted when the observable pattern described above (An AI response that positively exceeds the user's expectations in quality, depth...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0220 — The Gratitude Paradox
description: The paradoxical observation that some users thank an AI more genuinely than they thank people — because the fear of social evaluation is absent and gratitude can be expressed uninhibitedly. Related to AUG-0128 (The Gratitude Response), AUG-0241 (The Thank You Reflex), and AUG-0201 (The Proxy Closeness).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Gratitude Paradox' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The paradoxical observation that some users thank an AI more genuinely than they...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0305 — The Seasonal User
description: A user whose AI intensity fluctuates depending on life phase, project load, or season — sometimes intensive daily use, sometimes weeks-long pauses. Describes the observation that AI use need not be a linear process. Related to AUG-0141 (The Spectrum Framework), AUG-0120 (The Range Framework), and AUG-0316 (The Own Pace).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Seasonal User' is counted when the observable pattern described above (A user whose AI intensity fluctuates depending on life phase, project load, or s...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0330 — The Source Curiosity
alternateName (DE): Die Quellen-Neugier
description: The user's interest in tracing the origin and grounding of AI-generated statements — a productive curiosity about where information comes from, supporting informed evaluation of AI outputs.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Source Curiosity' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The user's interest in tracing the origin and grounding of AI-generated statemen...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0435 — The Dinner Shortcut
description: The quick AI query for everyday food decisions — "What can I cook with these ingredients?", "What fits as a side dish?" Describes the lowest-threshold form of AI use in the household. Related to AUG-0266 (The Recipe Riff), AUG-0251 (The Kitchen Table), and AUG-0373 (The Quick Check).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Dinner Shortcut' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The quick AI query for everyday food decisions — "What can I cook with these ing...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0520 — The Wiki Wormhole
description: The phenomenon where a simple AI question leads to a chain of follow-up questions that pulls the user deep into a topic — comparable to the "Wikipedia hole" but AI-driven. Related to AUG-0342 (The Curiosity Loop), AUG-0413 (The Infinite Scroll), and AUG-0458 (The Curiosity Drill).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Wiki Wormhole' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The phenomenon where a simple AI question leads to a chain of follow-up question...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0565 — The Balance Filter
description: The conscious strategy of balancing AI use and non-digital activities — through fixed times, rules, or routines that ensure AI enriches everyday life rather than dominating it. Related to AUG-0074 (Analog Anchors), AUG-0282 (The Dinner Table Pause), and Axiom 7 (The Return Principle).
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Balance Filter' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The conscious strategy of balancing AI use and non-digital activities — through ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0981 — The Companion Pattern
alternateName (DE): Das Begleiter-Muster
description: The pattern where users integrate AI assistance into daily routines as a steady companion for thinking, drafting, and exploration. Best experienced as complement to human reflection rather than substitute — co-presence rather than replacement.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Companion Pattern' is counted when the observable pattern described above (The pattern where users integrate AI assistance into daily routines as a steady ...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0007 — The Blending Effect [BLE]
discipline: KG | period: 2
description: Eigene Gedanken und KI-generierter Inhalt verbinden sich zu einem gemeinsamen Ergebnis. Ein Zuordnungsphaenomen, kein Verlustphaenomen.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'The Blending Effect' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Eigene Gedanken und KI-generierter Inhalt verbinden sich zu einem gemeinsamen Er...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).

discipline: KG | period: 2
description: Beobachtbare Zeitspanne, in der KI-generierte Information aktuell bleibt. Beschreibt ein Verfallsmuster.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Epistemic Half-Life' is counted when the observable pattern described above (Beobachtbare Zeitspanne, in der KI-generierte Information aktuell bleibt. Beschr...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.

### AUG-0478 — The Clarity Catalyst [CLC]

### AUG-0188 — Tone Alignment [TAL]
discipline: KM | period: 3
description: KI passt sich dem Kommunikationsstil des Nutzers an. Ueber Zeit entsteht ein gemeinsamer, produktiver Ton.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Tone Alignment' is counted when the observable pattern described above (KI passt sich dem Kommunikationsstil des Nutzers an. Ueber Zeit entsteht ein gem...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0161 — Invisible Colleague [INV]
discipline: RL | period: 3
description: KI als produktives Hintergrund-Werkzeug im Arbeitsalltag. Verfuegbar bei Bedarf, ohne soziale Dynamik.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Invisible Colleague' is counted when the observable pattern described above (KI als produktives Hintergrund-Werkzeug im Arbeitsalltag. Verfuegbar bei Bedarf,...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.
measurementSchema: Coding-scheme occurrence count; observer agreement (Cohen's κ).
author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)

### AUG-0981 — Companion Pattern [CPP]
discipline: RL | period: 3
description: KI als wiederkehrendes Arbeitswerkzeug. Beobachtbar etablieren sich individuelle Nutzungsmuster und bevorzugte Ablaeufe.
operationalDefinition: Operational scope: an instance of 'Companion Pattern' is counted when the observable pattern described above (KI als wiederkehrendes Arbeitswerkzeug. Beobachtbar etablieren sich individuelle...) occurs at least once within a defined interaction segment of N turns (typically N=10), as coded by trained raters.

author: Andreas Ehstand (ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796)
